name: Classic Eggs Benedict
servings: serves 4 | prep. time 10 minutes | cooking time 10-15 minutes

ingredients.item: Pinch of kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

method: In a large pot filled with water, add the white wine vinegar and bring to a gentle simmer. Don't let the water bubble too much.
Whisk the water with a spoon to make a whirl. Crack the eggs into the water.
Cook for 2-3 minutes. Remove the eggs from the pot with a slotted spoon and set aside.
To make the hollandaise sauce, prepare a small bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Crack the egg yolks into the pot. Whisk them until well-combined.
Stir in the lemon juice and vinegar. Constantly stirring, add the butter little by little until creamy and a thickened sauce is formed.
Season the sauce with salt and pepper and set aside.
Cook the bacon in a medium nonstick pan over high heat until crispy, about 5-6 minutes. Set aside.
Toast the English muffins. Once toasted, top each muffin with the sauce, bacon, and a cooked egg.
Top with the parsley before serving.
